commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix(model-fallback): classify Codex usage-limit payloads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Codex usage-limit error payloads can currently be treated as completed embedded-agent results instead of fallback-worthy provider failures.

`classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback()` already inspects embedded error payload text and calls `classifyFailoverReason()`. The shared matcher classifies usage-limit text as `rate_limit`, but `classifyBusinessDenialErrorPayloadReason()` only allowed `auth`, `auth_permanent`, and `billing`.

That means a Codex usage-limit payload can be recognized as `rate_limit` and then discarded by the embedded-result classifier, preventing configured model fallback from advancing at that boundary.

## What Changed

- Allows `rate_limit` as a fallback-safe embedded error-payload classification.
- Adds a guard on `finalAssistantVisibleText` so the classifier does not trigger fallback when the agent already delivered visible output to the user.
- Adds focused regression coverage for the Codex usage-limit payload.
- Keeps scope limited to embedded result classification.
- Does not change fallback ordering, auth profile selection, cooldown policy, or Codex app-server protocol handling.

## Evidence

Behavior tested: a Codex usage-limit payload should classify as `rate_limit` with code `embedded_error_payload`, instead of returning `null`.

Real environment: local OpenClaw checkout using production `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback()`.

### Before patch, upstream/main

Command:

`EXPECT_RATE_LIMIT=0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/__tmp_codex_usage_limit_real_proof.test.ts`

Observed terminal output:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/rev/projects/openclaw

stdout | src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/__tmp_codex_usage_limit_real_proof.test.ts > Codex usage-limit fallback real behavior proof > classifies the real Codex subscription usage-limit payload shape
codex_usage_limit_classification=null

 ✓  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/__tmp_codex_usage_limit_real_proof.test.ts (1 test) 93ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
   Start at  15:28:01
   Duration  1.09s (transform 936ms, setup 401ms, import 449ms, tests 93ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.56s
```

### After patch

Command:

`EXPECT_RATE_LIMIT=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/__tmp_codex_usage_limit_real_proof.test.ts`

Observed terminal output:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/rev/projects/openclaw

stdout | src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/__tmp_codex_usage_limit_real_proof.test.ts > Codex usage-limit fallback real behavior proof > classifies the real Codex subscription usage-limit payload shape
codex_usage_limit_classification={"message":"openai/gpt-5.5 ended with a provider error: You've reached your Codex subscription usage limit. Next reset in 32 minutes, Jun 20 at 3:44 PM EDT. Wait until the reset time, use another Codex account if available, or switch to another configured model/provider.","reason":"rate_limit","code":"embedded_error_payload","rawError":"You've reached your Codex subscription usage limit. Next reset in 32 minutes, Jun 20 at 3:44 PM EDT. Wait until the reset time, use another Codex account if available, or switch to another configured model/provider."}

 ✓  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/__tmp_codex_usage_limit_real_proof.test.ts (1 test) 40ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
   Start at  15:28:37
   Duration  506ms (transform 238ms, setup 162ms, import 183ms, tests 40ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.76s
```

### Focused validation after final amend

Commands:

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/codex-usage-limit-fallback-real-proof.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts`

`git diff --check`

Observed terminal output:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/rev/projects/openclaw

 ✓  unit-fast  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/codex-usage-limit-fallback-real-proof.test.ts (4 tests) 13ms
 ✓  unit-fast  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts (12 tests) 20ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  06:40:30
   Duration  1.44s (transform 1.87s, setup 0ms, import 2.43s, tests 33ms, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/rev/projects/openclaw

 ✓  agents  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts (81 tests) 5732ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  81 passed (81)
   Start at  06:40:35
   Duration  11.96s (transform 4.64s, setup 472ms, import 5.54s, tests 5.73s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 24.19s
```

`git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Real Behavior Proof

The permanent real behavior proof test is included as `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/codex-usage-limit-fallback-real-proof.test.ts`. It exercises the production `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback()` classifier with the exact Codex subscription usage-limit error text and verifies:

- Primary path: isError payload with usage-limit text produces `rate_limit` fallback
- Non-error payload: same text as normal assistant output produces no fallback
- Visible output already delivered: no fallback (user already has a response)
- Cross-provider: same text via openrouter produces `rate_limit` fallback

## Final Branch State

Final pushed commit: `b7e489643175ddb3336604de213be71e7354a6e0`.

## What Was Not Tested

I did not force a live Codex account into a usage-limit state. That would be flaky and account-state dependent. This proof exercises the deterministic fallback classification boundary using the production embedded result classifier.

A separate follow-up may still be needed if Codex emits this same banner as normal assistant text before it becomes an `isError` payload.

## Tests

AI-assisted: yes.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No narrow code repair remains from source review; maintainer review should decide whether to accept the intentional rate-limit fallback routing change.; Cleared: The diff changes classifier logic and tests only; it adds no dependencies, scripts, secrets handling, CI, package, or supply-chain surface. (sha=b7e489643175ddb3336604de213be71e7354a6e0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `rate_limit` to embedded error-payload fallback classification, guards already-visible final assistant text, and adds Codex usage-limit classifier regression proof tests.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +151. Total +159 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible with an embedded `isError` usage-limit payload: the shared matcher can return `rate_limit`, but the embedded classifier drops that reason before fallback can advance.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow code repair remains from source review; maintainer review should decide whether to accept the intentional rate-limit fallback routing change.
